commit.message:
Implement compliance release-evidence and protected sign-off APIs (#612)

* Initial plan

* Implement compliance release-evidence and protected sign-off APIs

- Add specific readiness states to SignOffReleaseReadinessStatus: BlockedMissingConfiguration, BlockedProviderUnavailable, BlockedMissingEvidence, DegradedStaleEvidence, NotReleaseEvidence
- Add IsReleaseEvidence field to ProtectedSignOffEvidencePack (derived from IsReleaseGrade)
- Add IsReleaseEvidence field to GetSignOffReleaseReadinessResponse
- Update GetReleaseReadinessAsync to return specific semantic states
- Fix ClassifyFreshness to use service time provider instead of DateTimeOffset.UtcNow
- Include isReleaseEvidence in ReleaseReadySignaled webhook event data
- Add 100 comprehensive tests (CRE01-CRE100) in ComplianceReleaseEvidenceTests.cs

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/scholtz/BiatecTokensApi/sessions/282dda86-b665-46f9-a541-0d03f163eb97

* Fix failing tests: reorder readiness status derivation and update assertions for new specific states

- Fix GetReleaseReadinessAsync: BlockedMissingEvidence triggers when freshnessStatus==Unavailable (not by blocker.All constraint)
- Fix NotReleaseEvidence: only triggered when hasApprovalWebhook==false (no webhooks at all)
- Fix CRE80 test assertion: use EqualTo(Ready) instead of Not.EqualTo(default)
- Update all existing ProtectedSignOff test assertions: Stale→DegradedStaleEvidence, Blocked→AnyOf(Blocked,BlockedMissingEvidence,...)

[loganalyzer] Ignore link-training status query errors during port transitions (#23200)

SAI cannot read link-training failure/rx status registers while the port
serdes is mid-transition (admin toggle). This causes transient ERR logs
from getPortLinkTrainingFailure and getPortLinkTrainingRxStatus in
orchagent that are harmless and do not indicate a real problem.

Once the port stabilizes (up or down), the queries succeed via the
periodic poller. Add both error patterns to the common ignore list.

Signed-off-by: Dashuai Zhang <dashuaizhang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix test_masked_services.py test when golden config is presented (#23183)

What is the motivation for this PR?
Some interfaces depend on physical-layer attributes in golden_config_db.json (e.g., link-training) to come up. Previously config_reload was called without override_config=True, so these settings were lost during minigraph load. This worked before but breaks on newer platforms that require these settings.

How did you do it?
Check if /etc/sonic/golden_config_db.json exists on the DUT. If yes, call config_reload with override_config=True; otherwise call without override to avoid failure.

How did you verify/test it?
Verified on physical testbed. Interfaces requiring link-training come up correctly when golden config is present.

Any platform specific information?
N/A

Signed-off-by: Dashuai Zhang <dashuaizhang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(dx): scoring script bug fixes + re-run against all 6 pilot conditions

Fixes applied:
1. grep -> Node.js fs scan (cross-platform, no shell grep dependency)
2. --passWithNoTests removed; test count checked explicitly (0 tests = fail)
3. Coverage parsed from jest --coverage output; threshold 60% on feature file
4. rm -rf -> fs.rmSync(recursive) (cross-platform cleanup)
5. git clone --depth 1 -> --depth 20 (commit history check sees real history)
6. Bounded: checks new feature route only (digest.ts / activity.ts), not all routes

Re-run results (corrected gradient):
  naive:      1/7 automatable (scaffold commit artifact; real forks expected 0)
  competent:  3/7
  gs:         7/7 (perfect on all automatable checks)

GS achieves ceiling on automatable checks. Composable +3 (live server) is the
only remaining gap before April session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: generative-specification
repository.description: Generative Specification: experiments, evidence, white paper, and community quality gates flywheel

commit.message:
feat(dx): add self-administered pilot results — baseline scores for April experiment

Six conditions piloted across both workshop repos:
- base-naive / kanban-naive: 0/14, Prisma in all routes, no tests
- base-competent / kanban-competent: 2-3/14, tests passing, no architecture
- base-gs / kanban-gs: 6-8/14, service layer, CLAUDE.md, ADRs, passing tests

Scoring script issues found (4 bugs, 2 calibration issues):
- Bounded check is a false positive on Windows (grep unavailable)
- Verifiable false positive when 0 tests + --passWithNoTests flag
- Coverage check not enforced in automation
- git --depth 1 makes commit format check unreliable

Rubric calibration: Bounded check fails for GS because pre-existing scaffold
routes carry Prisma. New feature routes (digest.ts, activity.ts) are clean.
Recommendation: check Bounded on new/changed files only.

All implementations in experiments/dx/pilot/. Results in results.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(paper): add §7.8 Meta-Application — autonomous specification evolution

New section covering:
- AX experiment as simultaneous ForgeCraft development mechanism
- close_cycle audit loop: AI identifies gap, AI encodes fix, human validates
- Human role as trigger/validator, not author (git history verifiable)
- Eventually convergent (not monotone) — v4 regression explained as gap interaction
- F3 dependency rebuttal: ForgeCraft 1.0 emerged from AX, not prior to it
- Self-applicability as strongest implication: pragmatic tier governs its own instrument

Cross-references: §4.2 (pragmatic tier), §7.7 (F3 threat), §9.1 (agentic self-refinement), §10 (community ratchet)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(paper): tighten F2/F4 -- formula as proposed model, non-software claims as hypotheses

F2: Added '(proposed model; analytically derived, N=3 directional support —
DX iteration-count data will provide first empirical validation)' parenthetical
at the §9.4 formula introduction. No formula changes — framing only.

F4: Changed 'the same principle governs any domain' to 'The same structural
argument applies — as an untested hypothesis — to any domain' (§10). Added
explicit framing sentence before the Convergent Principle examples:
'The following are structural analogies — the argument that the same principle
should apply, not evidence that it does.' Updated declarative language in the
medical/surgical/autonomous-vehicle paragraph to conditional ('would carry',
'would be', 'would operate', 'would approach', 'would be a surface', 'may
follow') per external reviewer note.

Changelog: F2/F4 framing tightened per external review noted as items (16)
and (17) in the v1.1 Changes from v1.0 section.

commit.message:
Add troubleshooting note for dbt debug logs in `ExecutionMode.WATCHER` (#2491)

Documents that dbt debug logs can be enabled by passing `--debug` via
`dbt_cmd_global_flags` in `operator_args`, and that
`AIRFLOW__LOGGING__LOGGING_LEVEL=DEBUG` must be set to make them visible
when running dbt as a subprocess.

Relates to: https://github.com/astronomer/astronomer-cosmos/issues/2317

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: astronomer-cosmos
repository.description: Run your dbt Core or dbt Fusion projects as Apache Airflow DAGs and Task Groups with a few lines of code

commit.message:
docs(white-paper): adversarial audit fixes — 17 findings addressed

FATAL fixes:
- F1: Named circularity disclosure added as explicit threat; external
  metrics (tsc/ESLint/npm audit) named as partial mitigation; third-party
  tool validation named as planned post-submission step
- F2/F10: Theoretical S vs S_realized proxy distinction added to §9.4;
  S_realized is product instrumentation, not independent measurement
- F3: Specification-authorship confound named explicitly; 'no other
  practitioner has applied GS as of this submission' stated directly
- F4: Post-hoc progression labeled 'iterated optimization on a single
  benchmark, not independent confirmation' in §7.7.B.1 lead

SIGNIFICANT fixes:
- F5: Chomsky analogy disclaimer strengthened; LLMs do not formally
  instantiate Type 1 grammars; formal apparatus not imported; intended
  for linguists to read without objection
- F6: §7.7.B.1 lead now explicitly states control beat GS v1 on
  Executable; GS v1 advantage was Composable only
- F7: 'independently reproduced' -> 'reproduced in a separate
  author-run session' (abstract)
- F8: Paradigm contingency nuance added; what may dissolve with stronger
  models vs what is system-level and survives
- F9: Morris inversion acknowledged; GS uses Morris as taxonomy while
  inverting its directionality
- F10: See F2/F10
- F11: AI auditor explicitly named; no inter-rater reliability disclosed
- F12: 'cannot regress' -> 'tends toward rising floor' with quality gate
  correctness caveat; closing spiral framing

MODERATE fixes:
- F13: 1957 claim reframed as retrospective observation, not established
  historiographic fact
- F14: Mutation gap named as GS v1 specification failure (constraint
  absent from template), not model failure
- F15: 'zero application code' claim given same caveat as active-hours
  (self-reported, git history records commits not authorship)
- F16: Domain dimensional expansion labeled as hypothesis with
  observational evidence; controlled experiment named as proposed
  follow-up; meta-ask inversion noted
- F17: Ceiling decimal values replaced with ordinal bands; S_aggregate
  quantitative prediction removed; false precision eliminated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(white-paper): three structural improvements — no DX required

    §4.1.c to immediately after the two-axes paragraph in §4.1.a,
    before §4.1.b. Now appears the moment the Chomsky/Martin axes
    are first described, not after two subsections that reference
    the concept.

(b) §7.7.B insight-first: added three-sentence lead paragraph at
    the top of §7.7.B.1 Results naming the three main findings
    upfront (naive-vs-structured boundary, 14/14 achievability,
    experiment-corrects-itself property). Tables and details now
    read as evidence for a stated story, not a puzzle to decode.

(c) About Author elevation: 'every trade at once' passage moved from
    last paragraph to first, so the section opens with the story
    that explains the paper rather than burying it after credentials.
    Doctoral speculation moved to closing position.

commit.message:
docs(white-paper): adversarial audit fixes — theorem→principle, pre-registration, FC COI, formula frame, Executable, BRAD taxonomy, supplement URL, Invellum scope, domain dimensional expansion

- Rename 'community convergence theorem' → 'community convergence principle' throughout (theorem requires formal proof; this is a structural argument)
- Replace 'pre-registered' with 'prospectively designed, verified by timestamped commits' + add repo URL
- Add ForgeCraft COI scope clarification: all 6 cases predate FC or used pre-release; methodology validity independent of the tool
- Reframe I∝(1-S)/S from 'deductive claim' → 'analytically derived theoretical frame'; add note that DX will collect left-side data (iteration counts per participant)
- Unify Executable emergence framing: 'implicit from beginning, articulated as formal property by the experiment'
- Add GS_Experiment_Supplement.md availability URL at first citation
- Qualify Invellum metrics table as qualitative case — no pre-specification baseline available
- Update BRAD taxonomy from vague 'AI-derived' to named 3-tier structure: 12 event types, 8 legal relevance tags, 7 behavioral pattern tags; jurisdiction-aware (MN statutes + all 50 states); statutory grounding noted
- Add 'Domain dimensional expansion' to glossary with full definition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
